title: Cirrus: Fix re-run of release task into no-op.
body:
This task depends upon other tasks caching their binaries.  If for
whatever reason the `release` task is re-run and/or is out-of-order
with it's dependents, the state of cache will be undefined. Previously
this would result in an error, and failing of the release task.
This commit alters this behavior to issue a warning instead.

---

The release-task ***must*** always execute last, in order to guarantee a
consistent cache of release archives from dependent tasks.  It
accomplishes this by verifying it's task-number matches one-less than
the total number of tasks.  Previous to this commit, a YAML anchor/alias
was used to avoid duplication of the dependency list between 'success'
and 'release'

However, it's been observed that this opens the possibility for
'release' and 'success' tasks to race when running on a PR.  Because
YAML anchor/aliases cannot be used to modify lists, duplication is
required to make 'release' actually depend upon 'success'.

This duplication will introduce an additional maintenance burden.
Though when adding a new task, it's already very easy to forget to
update the 'depends_on' list.  Assist both cases by the addition
unit-tests to verify ``.cirrus.yml`` dependency contents and structure.

comments.body: @mheon @rhatdan this will fix the 'release' task failing in case it is re-run or happens out-of-order.  Still investigating how/why this could be failing on PRs...
...I see the problem.  Fixing and writing a quick unit-test for it.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Yep working on that...I want to add a unit-test for `.cirrus.yml` to help prevent future related problems.

Summary:

    * The first commit will fix 'release' failing on master and PRs if someone hits the 're-run' button.
    * The second commit fixes a suspected race-condition that affects the release task only in PRs

I'm not 100% sure of the CI race-conditions existence or mechanism.  My conclusion was drawn based on the sudden onset of release-task failures in PRs, absent of other related changes.

See the commit messages for full explanations.

Full disclosure:

> It's possible we could simply ignore any failed status of the 'release' task.  However, this will blind us to repeated failures, causing the "latest" binary downloads we provide to no longer be current.
